weather_report:
Late morning in Subiaco arrives washed in clear light, the sky a polished blue with scarcely a smudge of cloud. The sun already feels sure of itself and, with the forecast holding steady and cloudless through the early afternoon, it will be a distinctly sunny day. Expect that sharp, high-noon brightness and a strong UV bite despite the season; the kind of day where colors look freshly painted and shadows stay crisp. It should feel comfortably warm in the sun and a touch cool in the shade, the sort of balance that invites lingering.

Dress in light layers you can peel back, with a brimmed hat, sunglasses, and a generous sweep of sunscreen. A slow walk to pick up bread and fruit will be pleasant, as will perching at a café table under an awning to read or talk without the wind fussing at your pages. Consider hanging laundry, tending a few pots, or taking a short amble through a leafy street around lunch, then ease into a quiet, shaded corner for the brightest hours.
